```{bash, eval = FALSE}
wget -O fantom5.hg38.LCL.rep2.CTSS.bed.gz 'https://fantom.gsc.riken.jp/5/datafiles/reprocessed/hg38_latest/basic/human.cell_line.hCAGE/B%2520lymphoblastoid%2520cell%2520line%253a%2520GM12878%2520ENCODE%252c%2520biol_rep2.CNhs12332.10822-111C3.hg38.nobarcode.ctss.bed.gz' && \
wget -O fantom5.hg38.LCL.rep3.CTSS.bed.gz 'https://fantom.gsc.riken.jp/5/datafiles/reprocessed/hg38_latest/basic/human.cell_line.hCAGE/B%2520lymphoblastoid%2520cell%2520line%253a%2520GM12878%2520ENCODE%252c%2520biol_rep3.CNhs12333.10823-111C4.hg38.nobarcode.ctss.bed.gz'
```

```{r fantom5_cage_bins}
library(data.table)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(ggplot2)
library(Rsamtools)

# -----------------------------
# Paths and parameters
# -----------------------------
annotation_dir <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations"
fantom_dir <- file.path(annotation_dir, "fantom5")
dir.create(fantom_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Raw files can be either in annotation_dir or annotation_dir/fantom5
pick_existing_path <- function(fname) {
  candidates <- c(file.path(fantom_dir, fname), file.path(annotation_dir, fname))
  hit <- candidates[file.exists(candidates)][1]
  if (is.na(hit)) stop(sprintf("Missing required file: %s", fname))
  hit
}

cageBedGzPath <- pick_existing_path("fantom5.hg38.CAGE_peaks.bed.gz")
lclRep1CtssBedGzPath <- pick_existing_path("fantom5.hg38.LCL.rep1.CTSS.bed.gz")
lclRep2CtssBedGzPath <- pick_existing_path("fantom5.hg38.LCL.rep2.CTSS.bed.gz")
lclRep3CtssBedGzPath <- pick_existing_path("fantom5.hg38.LCL.rep3.CTSS.bed.gz")

# Consensus output always goes here
lclCageBedGzPath <- file.path(fantom_dir, "fantom5.hg38.LCL.consensus.CAGE_peaks.bed.gz")

minReplicateSupport <- 2L
minTotalScore <- 30L
scoreToWrite <- "sum"  # "sum" or "max"
useStrandForExactMatch <- TRUE

sample_root <- "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results"
default_sample <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
default_chrom <- "chr1"
window_bp <- 2000L
bin_size <- 20L
n_score_bins <- 4L

# Directories/inputs consistent with prior chunks
fire_peak_dir <- file.path(sample_root, default_sample, "extracted_results/all_by_chr")
sample_dir <- file.path(sample_root, default_sample)
trees <- list.files(
  file.path(sample_dir, "extracted_results/nuc_by_chr"),
  pattern = sprintf("ft_extracted_nuc\\.%s\\.bed\\.gz$", default_chrom),
  full.names = TRUE
)
if (length(trees) == 0L) {
  stop(sprintf("No nucleosome BED files found for sample=%s chrom=%s", default_sample, default_chrom))
}

# -----------------------------
# Helper functions
# -----------------------------
readCtssBed6 <- function(ctssBedGzPath, replicateLabel) {
  ctssTable <- fread(cmd = paste("zcat", shQuote(ctssBedGzPath)), header = FALSE)
  setnames(ctssTable, c("chrom", "start", "end", "name", "score", "strand"))
  ctssTable[, `:=`(
    start = as.integer(start),
    end = as.integer(end),
    score = as.integer(score),
    replicate = replicateLabel
  )]
  ctssTable[]
}

buildConsensusCtss <- function(rep1Table, rep2Table, rep3Table,
                               minReplicateSupport, minTotalScore, scoreToWrite) {
  combinedCtss <- rbindlist(list(rep1Table, rep2Table, rep3Table), use.names = TRUE)
  combinedCtss[, cagePeakId := paste0(chrom, ":", start, "..", end, ",", strand)]

  consensusTable <- combinedCtss[
    , .(
      scoreSum = sum(score),
      scoreMax = max(score),
      nRep = sum(score > 0L)
    ),
    by = .(chrom, start, end, strand, cagePeakId)
  ][nRep >= minReplicateSupport & scoreSum >= minTotalScore]

  consensusTable[, scoreToUse := if (scoreToWrite == "max") scoreMax else scoreSum]

  bed9Table <- consensusTable[
    , .(
      chrom = chrom,
      peakStart = start,
      peakEnd = end,
      cagePeakId = cagePeakId,
      score = as.integer(scoreToUse),
      strand = strand,
      tssStart = start,
      tssEnd = end,
      itemRgb = "0,0,0"
    )
  ]
  setorder(bed9Table, chrom, peakStart, peakEnd, strand)
  bed9Table[]
}

writeBgzipAndTabix <- function(bed9Table, outputBedGzPath) {
  tempBedPath <- sub("\\.gz$", "", outputBedGzPath)
  fwrite(bed9Table, file = tempBedPath, sep = "\t", col.names = FALSE)
  Rsamtools::bgzip(tempBedPath, dest = outputBedGzPath, overwrite = TRUE)
  Rsamtools::indexTabix(outputBedGzPath, format = "bed")
  unlink(tempBedPath)
  invisible(outputBedGzPath)
}

# -----------------------------
# 1) Read all FANTOM5 CAGE peaks
# -----------------------------
allCageTable <- fread(cmd = paste("zcat", shQuote(cageBedGzPath)), header = FALSE)
setnames(allCageTable, c("chrom","peakStart","peakEnd","cagePeakId","score","strand","tssStart","tssEnd","itemRgb"))

# -----------------------------
# 2) Generate consensus file first, then load it from disk
# -----------------------------
rep1Ctss <- readCtssBed6(lclRep1CtssBedGzPath, "rep1")
rep2Ctss <- readCtssBed6(lclRep2CtssBedGzPath, "rep2")
rep3Ctss <- readCtssBed6(lclRep3CtssBedGzPath, "rep3")

lclConsensusBed9_to_write <- buildConsensusCtss(
  rep1Table = rep1Ctss,
  rep2Table = rep2Ctss,
  rep3Table = rep3Ctss,
  minReplicateSupport = minReplicateSupport,
  minTotalScore = minTotalScore,
  scoreToWrite = scoreToWrite
)
writeBgzipAndTabix(lclConsensusBed9_to_write, lclCageBedGzPath)

# Load consensus from written file (explicitly)
lclConsensusBed9 <- fread(cmd = paste("zcat", shQuote(lclCageBedGzPath)), header = FALSE)
setnames(lclConsensusBed9, c("chrom","peakStart","peakEnd","cagePeakId","score","strand","tssStart","tssEnd","itemRgb"))

# -----------------------------
# 3) Exact coordinate overlap summary
# -----------------------------
lclTssTable <- copy(lclConsensusBed9)[, .(
  chrom, tssStart, tssEnd, strand,
  lclScore = as.numeric(score),
  lclId = cagePeakId
)]
allTssTable <- copy(allCageTable)[, .(
  chrom, tssStart, tssEnd, strand,
  allScore = as.numeric(score),
  allId = cagePeakId
)]

if (!useStrandForExactMatch) {
  lclTssTable[, strand := "*"]
  allTssTable[, strand := "*"]
}

setkey(lclTssTable, chrom, tssStart, tssEnd, strand)
setkey(allTssTable, chrom, tssStart, tssEnd, strand)

exactMatchTable <- allTssTable[lclTssTable, nomatch = 0L]
lclOnlyExactTable <- lclTssTable[!allTssTable, on = .(chrom, tssStart, tssEnd, strand)]
exactOverlapSummary <- list(
  nLclCtss = nrow(lclTssTable),
  nAllTss = nrow(allTssTable),
  nExactMatches = nrow(exactMatchTable),
  fracLclWithExactMatch = nrow(unique(exactMatchTable[, .(chrom, tssStart, tssEnd, strand)])) / nrow(lclTssTable),
  nLclOnlyExact = nrow(lclOnlyExactTable)
)

# -----------------------------
# 4) TSS bins by score (selected chrom) + nucleosome profile
# uses helper functions from your {r define-functions} chunk:
#   - load_nucleosome_annotations()
#   - make_meta_normalized_TSS()
# -----------------------------
tss_bins <- copy(lclConsensusBed9)[chrom == default_chrom]
tss_bins[, score := as.numeric(score)]
tss_bins <- tss_bins[is.finite(score) & !is.na(score)]
if (nrow(tss_bins) == 0L) {
  stop(sprintf("No consensus TSS found on selected chromosome: %s", default_chrom))
}

nuc_gr <- load_nucleosome_annotations(trees)

tss_bins[, score_rank := frank(score, ties.method = "average")]
tss_bins[, scoreBinInt := pmax(1L, pmin(
  n_score_bins,
  as.integer(ceiling((score_rank / max(score_rank)) * n_score_bins))
))]
tss_bins[, scoreBin := factor(paste0("Q", scoreBinInt), levels = paste0("Q", seq_len(n_score_bins)))]

meta_counts <- rbindlist(
  lapply(paste0("Q", seq_len(n_score_bins)), function(bin_label) {
    tss_sub <- tss_bins[scoreBin == bin_label]
    if (nrow(tss_sub) == 0L) return(NULL)

    tss_gr_sub <- GRanges(
      seqnames = tss_sub$chrom,
      ranges = IRanges(start = tss_sub$tssStart + 1L, end = tss_sub$tssEnd),
      strand = tss_sub$strand
    )

    prof <- make_meta_normalized_TSS(
      nuc_gr = nuc_gr,
      tss_gr = tss_gr_sub,
      window_bp = window_bp,
      bin_size = bin_size,
      label = bin_label
    )
    prof[, nTss := length(tss_gr_sub)]
    prof
  }),
  fill = TRUE
)

if (nrow(meta_counts) == 0L) stop("No meta-profile points generated.")
setnames(meta_counts, c("bin", "N", "source"), c("rel_bin", "midpointsPerTss", "scoreBin"))
meta_counts[, scoreBin := factor(scoreBin, levels = paste0("Q", seq_len(n_score_bins)))]
setorder(meta_counts, scoreBin, rel_bin)

plot_title <- sprintf(
  "Nucleosome midpoint profiles around LCL consensus CAGE TSS bins (%s, %s)",
  default_sample, default_chrom
)

fantom5_nuc_plot <- ggplot(meta_counts, aes(x = rel_bin, y = midpointsPerTss, color = scoreBin)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.95) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-window_bp, window_bp, by = 500L),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Distance to TSS",
    y = "Nucleosome midpoints per TSS",
    color = "CAGE score bin",
    title = plot_title,
    subtitle = sprintf("Bins: Q1 (lowest) to Q%d (highest); window = +/- %d bp", n_score_bins, window_bp)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

print(fantom5_nuc_plot)

plot_dir <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/nuc"
dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    plot_dir,
    sprintf("fantom5_LCL_cage_score_bins_nucleosome_midpoints_%s_%s.pdf", default_sample, default_chrom)
  ),
  plot = fantom5_nuc_plot,
  width = 8.5, height = 5.5
)

list(
  consensusBedGz = lclCageBedGzPath,
  consensusTabix = paste0(lclCageBedGzPath, ".tbi"),
  overlapSummary = exactOverlapSummary,
  firePeakDir = fire_peak_dir,
  treesUsed = trees,
  nNucMidpoints = length(nuc_gr),
  nTssUsed = nrow(tss_bins)
)


```


# add m6a, msp and cpg from fiber-seq paper to the above analysis, see if those features correlate with expression and/or nucleosome positioning around TSS

/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results/AL10_bc2178_19130/extracted_results/cpg_by_chr
/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results/AL10_bc2178_19130/extracted_results/m6a_by_chr
/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results/AL10_bc2178_19130/extracted_resulta/msp_by_chr

sample = AL10_bc2178_19130
chr = 1

/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/code/expr_stratified_TSS_profiles_with_fiber_features.r
/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/code/run_expr_stratified_TSS_profiles_with_fiber_features.sh
```{r expr_stratified_TSS_profiles_with_fiber_features}
source("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/plot_nuc_distribution_functions.R")

gencode_tss_path <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations/TSS.gencode.v49.bed"
cage_tss_path    <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations/fantom5/fantom5.hg38.LCL.consensus.CAGE_peaks.withGene.bed.gz"
sample_root      <- "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results"
expr_path        <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations/ENCFF873VWU.gene_quan.tsv"
default_sample   <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
default_chrom    <- "chr1"
window_bp        <- 2000L
bin_size         <- 10L

source_colors <- c("GENCODE" = "#1f77b4", "CAGE" = "#be1c54ff")
expr_colors   <- c("high" = "#FF7F0E", "low" = "#2CA02C")
feature_colors <- c(
  "Nucleosome" = "#4D4D4D",
  "m6A"        = "#7B3294",
  "CpG"        = "#8C510A",
  "MSP"        = "#1B9E77"
)
feature_levels <- c("Nucleosome", "m6A", "CpG", "MSP")

sample_extract_dir <- file.path(sample_root, default_sample, "extracted_results")
feature_files <- list(
  Nucleosome = file.path(sample_extract_dir, "nuc_by_chr",
                         sprintf("%s.ft_extracted_nuc.%s.bed.gz", default_sample, default_chrom)),
  m6A        = file.path(sample_extract_dir, "m6a_by_chr",
                         sprintf("%s.ft_extracted_m6a.%s.bed.gz", default_sample, default_chrom)),
  CpG        = file.path(sample_extract_dir, "cpg_by_chr",
                         sprintf("%s.ft_extracted_cpg.%s.bed.gz", default_sample, default_chrom)),
  MSP        = file.path(sample_extract_dir, "msp_by_chr",
                         sprintf("%s.ft_extracted_msp.%s.bed.gz", default_sample, default_chrom))
)
if (!all(file.exists(unlist(feature_files)))) {
  stop("Missing one or more feature BED12 files for ", default_sample, " ", default_chrom)
}

feature_grs <- list(
  Nucleosome = load_fiber_feature_positions(feature_files[["Nucleosome"]], feature_name = "Nucleosome"),
  m6A        = load_fiber_feature_positions(feature_files[["m6A"]],        feature_name = "m6A"),
  CpG        = load_fiber_feature_positions(feature_files[["CpG"]],        feature_name = "CpG"),
  MSP        = load_fiber_feature_positions(feature_files[["MSP"]],        feature_name = "MSP")
)

annotate_gene_id_base <- function(gr) {
  mcols(gr)$gene_id_base <- sub("\\..*$", "", mcols(gr)$gene_id)
  gr
}

expr <- fread(expr_path)
expr <- expr[grepl("^ENSG", gene_id)]
expr[, gene_id_base := sub("\\..*$", "", gene_id)]
qs <- quantile(expr$TPM, probs = c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
expr[, expr_group := fcase(
  TPM >= qs[2], "high",
  TPM <= qs[1], "low",
  default = NA_character_
)]
expr <- expr[!is.na(expr_group), .(gene_id, gene_id_base, TPM, expr_group)]

tss_sets <- list(
  GENCODE = annotate_gene_id_base(load_tss_annotations(gencode_tss_path)),
  CAGE    = annotate_gene_id_base(load_tss_annotations_bed9_with_gene_id(cage_tss_path))
)

align_tss <- function(gr, source) {
  idx_full <- match(mcols(gr)$gene_id, expr$gene_id)
  idx_base <- match(mcols(gr)$gene_id_base, expr$gene_id_base)
  idx <- ifelse(!is.na(idx_full), idx_full, idx_base)
  valid <- !is.na(idx)
  if (!any(valid)) return(NULL)
  gr2 <- gr[valid]
  mcols(gr2)$expr_group <- expr$expr_group[idx[valid]]
  mcols(gr2)$TPM <- expr$TPM[idx[valid]]
  list(source = source, gr = gr2)
}

tss_by_group <- Filter(
  Negate(is.null),
  lapply(names(tss_sets), function(source) align_tss(tss_sets[[source]], source))
)

matched_sources <- vapply(tss_by_group, function(x) x$source, character(1))
if (!all(c("GENCODE", "CAGE") %in% matched_sources)) {
  stop("One or more TSS sources did not match the expression table; check CAGE gene_id annotation.")
}

meta_raw_list <- list()
meta_norm_list <- list()

for (entry in tss_by_group) {
  for (grp in c("high", "low")) {
    subset_gr <- entry$gr[mcols(entry$gr)$expr_group == grp]
    if (!length(subset_gr)) next

    for (feature_nm in names(feature_grs)) {
      raw_dt <- make_position_meta(
        feature_grs[[feature_nm]], subset_gr,
        window_bp = window_bp, bin_size = bin_size,
        normalize_by_tss = FALSE
      )
      raw_dt[, `:=`(feature = feature_nm, source = entry$source, expr_group = grp)]
      meta_raw_list[[length(meta_raw_list) + 1L]] <- raw_dt

      norm_dt <- make_position_meta(
        feature_grs[[feature_nm]], subset_gr,
        window_bp = window_bp, bin_size = bin_size,
        normalize_by_tss = TRUE
      )
      norm_dt[, `:=`(feature = feature_nm, source = entry$source, expr_group = grp)]
      meta_norm_list[[length(meta_norm_list) + 1L]] <- norm_dt
    }
  }
}

meta_raw <- rbindlist(meta_raw_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
meta_norm <- rbindlist(meta_norm_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

for (dt in list(meta_raw, meta_norm)) {
  dt[, source := factor(source, levels = c("GENCODE", "CAGE"))]
  dt[, expr_group := factor(expr_group, levels = c("high", "low"))]
  dt[, feature := factor(feature, levels = feature_levels)]
}

x_scale <- scale_x_continuous(
  breaks = seq(-window_bp, window_bp, 500),
  labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")
)

raw_source_plot <- ggplot(meta_raw, aes(x = bin, y = N, color = source)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.06, se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(feature ~ expr_group, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = source_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  x_scale +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Distance to TSS",
    y = "Feature midpoints per 10 bp bin",
    color = "TSS source",
    title = "Fiber-seq features around TSS: raw counts by expression group",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chromosome: %s", default_sample, default_chrom)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

norm_source_plot <- ggplot(meta_norm, aes(x = bin, y = N, color = source)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.06, se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(feature ~ expr_group, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = source_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  x_scale +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Distance to TSS",
    y = "Average feature midpoints per TSS (10 bp bins)",
    color = "TSS source",
    title = "Fiber-seq features around TSS: per-TSS normalized profiles",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chromosome: %s", default_sample, default_chrom)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

raw_expr_plot <- ggplot(meta_raw, aes(x = bin, y = N, color = expr_group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.06, se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(feature ~ source, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = expr_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  x_scale +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Distance to TSS",
    y = "Feature midpoints per 10 bp bin",
    color = "Expression bin",
    title = "Fiber-seq features around TSS: raw counts by TSS source",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chromosome: %s", default_sample, default_chrom)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

norm_expr_plot <- ggplot(meta_norm, aes(x = bin, y = N, color = expr_group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.06, se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(feature ~ source, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = expr_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  x_scale +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Distance to TSS",
    y = "Average feature midpoints per TSS (10 bp bins)",
    color = "Expression bin",
    title = "Fiber-seq features around TSS: normalized by TSS source",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chromosome: %s", default_sample, default_chrom)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

meta_z <- copy(meta_norm)
meta_z[, z := if (sd(N) == 0) 0 else as.numeric(scale(N)),
       by = .(feature, source, expr_group)]

shape_overlay_plot <- ggplot(meta_z, aes(x = bin, y = z, color = feature)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(expr_group ~ source) +
  scale_color_manual(values = feature_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  x_scale +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Distance to TSS",
    y = "Z-scored normalized signal",
    color = "Feature",
    title = "Relative positioning of nucleosome, m6A, CpG, and MSP signals",
    subtitle = "Z-scores are computed within each feature, TSS source, and expression group"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

safe_cor <- function(x, y, method = "pearson") {
  if (length(unique(x)) < 2L || length(unique(y)) < 2L) return(NA_real_)
  suppressWarnings(cor(x, y, method = method))
}

profile_corr <- rbindlist(lapply(setdiff(feature_levels, "Nucleosome"), function(feature_nm) {
  merged <- merge(
    meta_norm[feature == "Nucleosome", .(bin, source, expr_group, nuc_signal = N)],
    meta_norm[feature == feature_nm,   .(bin, source, expr_group, feature_signal = N)],
    by = c("bin", "source", "expr_group"),
    all = FALSE
  )
  merged[, .(
    pearson = safe_cor(nuc_signal, feature_signal, method = "pearson"),
    spearman = safe_cor(nuc_signal, feature_signal, method = "spearman")
  ), by = .(source, expr_group)][, feature := feature_nm]
}), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

profile_corr[, feature := factor(feature, levels = c("m6A", "CpG", "MSP"))]
corr_plot <- ggplot(profile_corr, aes(x = source, y = feature, fill = spearman)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", spearman)), size = 3.5) +
  facet_wrap(~expr_group, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#b2182b",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), na.value = "grey90") +
  labs(
    x = "TSS source",
    y = "Feature",
    fill = "Spearman\nrho",
    title = "Correlation between nucleosome and feature profiles across TSS bins",
    subtitle = "Positive values indicate co-enrichment in the same TSS-relative bins"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

feature_window_signal <- meta_norm[, .(signal_per_tss = sum(N)),
                                   by = .(feature, source, expr_group)]
expr_enrichment <- dcast(
  feature_window_signal,
  feature + source ~ expr_group,
  value.var = "signal_per_tss",
  fill = 0
)
expr_enrichment[, log2_high_vs_low := log2((high + 1e-6) / (low + 1e-6))]
expr_enrichment[, feature := factor(feature, levels = feature_levels)]

expr_enrichment_plot <- ggplot(expr_enrichment, aes(x = source, y = feature, fill = log2_high_vs_low)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", log2_high_vs_low)), size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2CA02C", mid = "white", high = "#FF7F0E",
                       midpoint = 0) +
  labs(
    x = "TSS source",
    y = "Feature",
    fill = "log2(high/low)",
    title = "Expression association of fiber-seq features within +/-2 kb of TSS",
    subtitle = "Computed from per-TSS normalized signal summed across bins"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(raw_source_plot)
print(norm_source_plot)
print(raw_expr_plot)
print(norm_expr_plot)
print(shape_overlay_plot)
print(corr_plot)
print(expr_enrichment_plot)

plot_dir <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/nuc"
table_dir <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/tables/nuc"
dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(table_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_raw_by_source_expr.pdf",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       plot = raw_source_plot, width = 10, height = 10)

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_norm_by_source_expr.pdf",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       plot = norm_source_plot, width = 10, height = 10)

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_raw_by_expr_source.pdf",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       plot = raw_expr_plot, width = 10, height = 10)

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_norm_by_expr_source.pdf",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       plot = norm_expr_plot, width = 10, height = 10)

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_zscore_overlay.pdf",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       plot = shape_overlay_plot, width = 8, height = 5)

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_vs_nucleosome_correlation_heatmap.pdf",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       plot = corr_plot, width = 7, height = 4)

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_expression_heatmap.pdf",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       plot = expr_enrichment_plot, width = 7, height = 4.5)

fwrite(meta_raw,
       file.path(table_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_raw_profiles.tsv",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       sep = "\t")

fwrite(meta_norm,
       file.path(table_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_norm_profiles.tsv",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       sep = "\t")

fwrite(profile_corr,
       file.path(table_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_vs_nucleosome_correlations.tsv",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       sep = "\t")

fwrite(expr_enrichment,
       file.path(table_dir,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_fiber_features_high_vs_low_expression.tsv",
                         default_sample, default_chrom)),
       sep = "\t")

```

# Offset from TSS to closest nucleosome midpoint 

```{r find_closest_nucleosomes_to_TSS}
source("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/plot_nuc_distribution_functions.R")


# ---- Parameters ----
sample_root    <- "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results"
default_sample <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
default_chrom  <- "chr1"
window_bp      <- 2000L
cage_tss_path  <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations/fantom5/fantom5.hg38.LCL.consensus.CAGE_peaks.bed.gz"
n_ranks        <- 5L

# ---- Load nucleosome BED12 ----
# Each row = one sequencing read (molecule); blocks within the read = nucleosomes
nuc_by_chr_dir <- file.path(sample_root, default_sample, "extracted_results/nuc_by_chr")
pattern  <- sprintf("ft_extracted_nuc\\.%s\\.bed\\.gz$", default_chrom)
nuc_file <- list.files(nuc_by_chr_dir, pattern = pattern, full.names = TRUE)[1]
stopifnot(!is.na(nuc_file), file.exists(nuc_file))

bed12 <- fread(
  nuc_file,
  sep = "\t", header = FALSE, select = 1:12,
  col.names = c("chrom","chromStart","chromEnd","name","score","strand",
                "thickStart","thickEnd","itemRgb","blockCount","blockSizes","blockStarts")
)

# ---- Step 1: Build a GRanges of reads (one row per molecule) ----
# Analog of fire.gr in Kevin's get_nearest_nuc_to_snp:
#   Kevin uses fire.gr (reads) to findOverlaps with SNPs;
#   here we use reads_gr to findOverlaps with TSS windows.
# 'name' is the read ID (RID) — same as Kevin's RID field.
reads_gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = bed12$chrom,
  ranges   = IRanges(bed12$chromStart + 1L, bed12$chromEnd),
  strand   = "*",
  RID      = bed12$name
)

# ---- Step 2: Build per-nucleosome table keyed by read ID ----
# calculate_nucleosome_midpoints parses BED12 blocks -> one row per nucleosome.
# 'name' (= RID) is carried through in the grouping key.
# nuc_mid is 0-based (raw BED arithmetic); we add 1L later to match GRanges 1-based coords
# midpoint is calculated as the average of the nucleosome start and end coordinates
nucs <- calculate_nucleosome_midpoints(bed12)
# rename name to RID for joining with reads_gr and TSS overlaps later
setnames(nucs, "name", "RID") 
# keep only read ID, nucleosome midpoint and size
nuc_read_cols <- nucs[, .(RID, nuc_mid, nuc_size)]

# ---- Step 3: Load TSS GRanges and restrict to working chromosome ----
tss_gr <- load_tss_annotations_bed9(cage_tss_path)
tss_gr <- tss_gr[as.character(seqnames(tss_gr)) == default_chrom]

# TSS lookup table for joining (tss_idx = integer index into tss_gr)
tss_dt <- data.table(
  # assign each TSS a unique index
  tss_idx    = seq_along(tss_gr),
  tss_pos    = start(tss_gr),            # 1-based (GRanges convention)
  tss_strand = as.character(strand(tss_gr))
)

# ---- Step 4: Find reads that overlap each TSS ± window ----
# Analog of: hits_snp <- findOverlaps(fire.gr, snps.gr)
# For each TSS, expand a window of ±window_bp; find all reads whose span overlaps it.
tss_windows_gr <- promoters(tss_gr, upstream = window_bp, downstream = window_bp + 1L)
hits_tss <- findOverlaps(reads_gr, tss_windows_gr)

# One row per (read, TSS) overlap
read_tss_pairs <- data.table(
  RID     = reads_gr$RID[queryHits(hits_tss)], # read ID for each overlap
  tss_idx = subjectHits(hits_tss)               # index of the TSS in tss_gr that overlaps the read
)

# ---- Step 5: For each (read, TSS) pair, expand to all nucleosomes on that read ----
# Analog of Kevin's inner_join(snp_df, nuc_df, by = "RID", relationship = "many-to-many"):
#   each read may have many nucleosomes, and each read may overlap many TSS.
rtp_nuc <- merge(read_tss_pairs, nuc_read_cols, by = "RID", allow.cartesian = TRUE) # for each read-TSS pair, get all nucleosomes on that read
rtp_nuc <- merge(rtp_nuc, tss_dt, by = "tss_idx", allow.cartesian = TRUE)

# ---- Step 6: Compute strand-aware offset from TSS to nucleosome midpoint ----
# nuc_mid is 0-based; add 1L to convert to 1-based before comparing with tss_pos (1-based).
# On + strand: positive rel_pos = nucleosome is downstream of TSS.
# On - strand: flip sign so rel_pos is always in TSS-centric orientation
#              (negative = upstream of TSS regardless of genomic strand).
# Analog of Kevin's dist_nuc_mid, but signed rather than absolute.
rtp_nuc[, rel_pos  := fifelse(tss_strand == "+",
                               (nuc_mid + 1L) - tss_pos,
                               tss_pos - (nuc_mid + 1L))]
rtp_nuc[, abs_dist := abs(rel_pos)]
rtp_nuc[, direction := fifelse(rel_pos < 0L, "upstream", "downstream")]

# ---- Step 7: Rank nucleosomes by proximity within each (TSS, read, direction) ----
# Analog of Kevin's slice_min(dist_nuc_mid, n=1) for the single nearest nuc,
# extended here to keep the top n_ranks per direction.
# rank 1 = nucleosome closest to the TSS on that read in that direction.
setorder(rtp_nuc, tss_idx, RID, direction, abs_dist)
rtp_nuc[, rank := seq_len(.N), by = .(tss_idx, RID, direction)]
rtp_nuc <- rtp_nuc[rank <= n_ranks]

rtp_nuc[, signed_rank   := fifelse(direction == "upstream", -rank, rank)]
rtp_nuc[, peak_adjacent := rank == 1L]
rtp_nuc[, peak_adjacent_label := ifelse(peak_adjacent, "Closest to TSS (rank 1)", "Ranks 2–5")]
rtp_nuc[, peak_adjacent_label := factor(peak_adjacent_label, 
                                        levels = c("Closest to TSS (rank 1)", "Ranks 2–5"))]

# ---- Plot: offset distribution by rank ----
# Each observation is one (read × TSS) data point, not an aggregate.
# This tests whether rank-1 nucleosomes (closest to TSS) have smaller,
# tighter offsets compared to ranks 2–5 on the same reads.
# Use coord_cartesian to zoom into the bulk of the data while preserving outlier-checking
offset_plot <- ggplot(rtp_nuc, aes(x = factor(signed_rank), y = abs_dist,
                                    fill = peak_adjacent_label)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 1.5, outlier.color = "grey50", outlier.alpha = 0.35) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Closest to TSS (rank 1)" = "#6a51a3", "Ranks 2–5" = "gray70")
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, quantile(rtp_nuc$abs_dist, 0.95))) +
  labs(
    x     = "Nucleosome rank (upstream = negative, downstream = positive)",
    y     = "Distance to TSS midpoint (bp)",
    fill  = NULL,
    title = sprintf("Per-read nearest nucleosomes to CAGE TSS (%s, %s)",
                    default_sample, default_chrom),
    subtitle = sprintf("Each point = one read × TSS pair; window = ±%d bp (y-axis limited to 95th percentile)", window_bp)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(offset_plot)

# ---- Plot: nucleosome size distributions by direction ----
upstream_size_hist <- ggplot(rtp_nuc %>% filter(direction == "upstream", !is.na(nuc_size), nuc_size < 1000),
                             aes(x = nuc_size)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "lightblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  labs(title = "Upstream nucleosome size distribution (per-read)",
       x = "Nucleosome size (bp)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

downstream_size_hist <- ggplot(rtp_nuc %>% filter(direction == "downstream", !is.na(nuc_size), nuc_size < 1000),
                               aes(x = nuc_size)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "lightcoral", color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  labs(title = "Downstream nucleosome size distribution (per-read)",
       x = "Nucleosome size (bp)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

print(upstream_size_hist)
print(downstream_size_hist)

plot_dir <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/nuc/offset_per_read_TSS"
dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  file.path(plot_dir,
            sprintf("TSS_nearest_nuc_per_read_%s_%s.pdf", default_sample, default_chrom)),
  plot = offset_plot, width = 10, height = 7
)

ggsave(
  file.path(plot_dir,
            sprintf("TSS_nearest_nuc_upstream_size_hist_per_read_%s_%s.pdf", default_sample, default_chrom)),
  plot = upstream_size_hist, width = 6, height = 4
)

ggsave(
  file.path(plot_dir,
            sprintf("TSS_nearest_nuc_downstream_size_hist_per_read_%s_%s.pdf", default_sample, default_chrom)),
  plot = downstream_size_hist, width = 6, height = 4
)


# perfomr wilcoxon test to see if rank 1 nucleosomes are significantly closer to TSS than ranks 2-5
wilcox_test <- wilcox.test(abs_dist ~ peak_adjacent_label, data = rtp_nuc)
wilcox_test_result <- data.table(
  statistic = wilcox_test$statistic,
  p_value = wilcox_test$p.value,
  method = wilcox_test$method,
  alternative = wilcox_test$alternative
)

# Build comparison pairs: upstream ranks vs -1, downstream ranks vs 1
# (rank_1 covers both -1 and 1, so split by direction)
other_srs        <- rtp_nuc[peak_adjacent == FALSE, sort(unique(signed_rank))]
upstream_comps   <- lapply(other_srs[other_srs < 0], function(r) c("-1", as.character(r)))
downstream_comps <- lapply(other_srs[other_srs > 0], function(r) c("1",  as.character(r)))
all_comps        <- c(upstream_comps, downstream_comps)

# Pre-filter to only significant pairs (p < 0.05) so ns brackets are hidden
sig_comps <- Filter(function(pair) {
  g1 <- rtp_nuc[as.character(signed_rank) == pair[1], abs_dist]
  g2 <- rtp_nuc[as.character(signed_rank) == pair[2], abs_dist]
  wilcox.test(g1, g2)$p.value < 0.05
}, all_comps)

# Expand y ceiling to accommodate stacked brackets without clipping
y_ceil <- quantile(rtp_nuc$abs_dist, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE) * (1 + 0.08 * length(sig_comps))

# Rebuild plot with expanded ylim (avoids replacing coord_cartesian mid-chain)
offset_plot_annotated <- ggplot(rtp_nuc, aes(x = factor(signed_rank), y = abs_dist,
                                              fill = peak_adjacent_label)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 1.5, outlier.color = "grey50", outlier.alpha = 0.35) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Closest to TSS (rank 1)" = "#6a51a3", "Ranks 2–5" = "gray70")
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, y_ceil)) +
  labs(
    x     = "Nucleosome rank (upstream = negative, downstream = positive)",
    y     = "Distance to TSS midpoint (bp)",
    fill  = NULL,
    title = sprintf("Per-read nearest nucleosomes to CAGE TSS (%s, %s)",
                    default_sample, default_chrom),
    subtitle = sprintf("Each point = one read × TSS pair; window = ±%d bp (y-axis limited to 95th percentile)", window_bp)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  ggsignif::geom_signif(
    comparisons      = sig_comps,
    test             = "wilcox.test",
    map_signif_level = TRUE,
    step_increase    = 0.08,
    tip_length       = 0.01,
    color            = "red",
    textsize         = 3.5
  )

print(offset_plot_annotated)
```

# Plot FIRE, m6A, CpG on a genome wide scale

Genome-wide track plots showing:

- **FIRE score** (`all.percent.accessible.bw`) — fraction of reads with a FIRE element
- **m6A / MSP fraction** — derived from `fire_all.bed.gz` (MSP + FIRE element bases / total covered bases per bin)
- **CpG methylation** (`*_CPG.combined.bw`) — average CpG methylation fraction per bin
- **Nucleosome coverage** (`all.nucleosome.coverage.bw`)
- **FIRE peaks** annotated as tick marks below the FIRE track

```{r genome_wide_setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## ── Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(GenomicRanges)
library(IRanges)
library(rtracklayer)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)
library(patchwork)   # multi-panel layout
library(scales)      # axis formatting

## ── Paths ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sample        <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
fire_root     <- "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results"
cpg_root      <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/fire_CpG"
chrom_sizes_f <- "/project/spott/reference/human/GRCh38/hg38.chrom.sizes"

sample_fire_dir <- file.path(fire_root, sample)
bw_dir          <- file.path(sample_fire_dir, "trackHub-v0.1", "bw")
extract_dir     <- file.path(sample_fire_dir, "extracted_results")

bw_fire    <- file.path(bw_dir, "all.percent.accessible.bw")
bw_nuc     <- file.path(bw_dir, "all.nucleosome.coverage.bw")
bw_cpg     <- file.path(cpg_root, sample, paste0(sample, "_CPG.combined.bw"))
peaks_file <- file.path(sample_fire_dir,
                        paste0(sample, "-fire-v0.1-peaks.bed.gz"))
fire_all_f <- file.path(extract_dir,
                        paste0(sample, ".fire_all.bed.gz"))
```


Tiles the genome into 100 kb bins, imports FIRE, nucleosome, and CpG bigwigs
```{r genome_wide_bins, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## ── Select chromosome ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Change plot_chr to any canonical chromosome to switch the view
plot_chr <- "chr1"

BIN_SIZE <- 100e3

chrom_sizes <- fread(chrom_sizes_f, col.names = c("chrom", "size"))
chr_size    <- chrom_sizes[chrom == plot_chr, size]
stopifnot(length(chr_size) == 1)

## Build bins for the selected chromosome only
chr_gr  <- GRanges(plot_chr, IRanges(1, chr_size))
seqlengths(chr_gr) <- setNames(chr_size, plot_chr)

bins_gr <- tileGenome(seqlengths(chr_gr),
                      tilewidth  = BIN_SIZE,
                      cut.last.tile.in.chrom = TRUE)

## Helper: summarise a BigWig into bins for the selected chromosome.
## binnedAverage requires names(RleList) == seqlevels(bins) exactly,
## so we extract only the one chromosome and rebuild a matching RleList.
binnedBW <- function(bw_path, bins) {
  chr <- seqlevels(bins)                         # single chromosome name
  bw  <- import(bw_path, which = bins, as = "RleList")
  rle <- if (!is.null(bw[[chr]])) bw[[chr]] else Rle(0, seqlengths(bins)[chr])
  binnedAverage(bins, RleList(setNames(list(rle), chr)), "score")
}

message("Importing FIRE percent-accessible bigwig for ", plot_chr, " …")
bins_fire <- binnedBW(bw_fire, bins_gr)

message("Importing nucleosome coverage bigwig for ", plot_chr, " …")
bins_nuc    <- binnedBW(bw_nuc,  bins_gr)

message("Importing linker/MSP coverage bigwig for ", plot_chr, " …")
bw_linker   <- file.path(bw_dir, "all.linker.coverage.bw")
bins_linker <- binnedBW(bw_linker, bins_gr)

message("Importing FIRE element coverage bigwig for ", plot_chr, " …")
bw_fire_cov   <- file.path(bw_dir, "all.fire.coverage.bw")
bins_fire_cov <- binnedBW(bw_fire_cov, bins_gr)

message("Importing CpG methylation bigwig for ", plot_chr, " …")
bins_cpg  <- binnedBW(bw_cpg,  bins_gr)
```

```{r genome_wide_m6a, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## ── Derive per-bin m6A and nucleosome fractions from coverage bigwigs ─────────
## Three mutually exclusive element classes per read:
##   FIRE element       (all.fire.coverage.bw)     → open, m6A-accessible
##   Linker / MSP       (all.linker.coverage.bw)   → open, m6A-accessible
##   Nucleosome         (all.nucleosome.coverage.bw) → closed
##
## Each bigwig score is a per-bin average coverage fraction after
## binnedAverage() summarises the signal across the 100 kb window.
## Let:
##   fc = all.fire.coverage.bw score in the bin
##   lc = all.linker.coverage.bw score in the bin
##   nc = all.nucleosome.coverage.bw score in the bin
##   tot = fc + lc + nc
##
## Then:
##   m6A fraction = (fc + lc) / tot
##   nucleosome fraction = nc / tot
##
## These are fractions, so the expected range is 0 to 1.

fc   <- bins_fire_cov$score
lc   <- bins_linker$score
nc   <- bins_nuc$score
tot  <- fc + lc + nc

bins_gr$m6a <- ifelse(tot > 0, (fc + lc) / tot, NA_real_)
bins_gr$nuc_frac <- ifelse(tot > 0, nc / tot, NA_real_)
```

```{r genome_wide_peaks, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## ── Load FIRE peaks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
peaks_dt <- fread(
  cmd       = paste("zcat", peaks_file),
  sep       = "\t",
  col.names = c("chr","peak_start","peak_end","start","end",
                "coverage","fire_coverage","score","nuc_coverage",
                "msp_coverage","coverage_H1","fire_coverage_H1","score_H1",
                "nuc_coverage_H1","msp_coverage_H1","coverage_H2",
                "fire_coverage_H2","score_H2","nuc_coverage_H2",
                "msp_coverage_H2","FDR","log_FDR","FIRE_size_mean",
                "FIRE_size_ssd","FIRE_start_ssd","FIRE_end_ssd",
                "local_max_count","peak_length","pass_coverage")
)[chr == plot_chr]

peaks_gr <- GRanges(peaks_dt$chr,
                    IRanges(peaks_dt$peak_start + 1, peaks_dt$peak_end),
                    score    = peaks_dt$score,
                    log_FDR  = peaks_dt$log_FDR,
                    pass_cov = peaks_dt$pass_coverage)
```

```{r genome_wide_tidy, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## ── Tidy data frame for ggplot2 ──────────────────────────────────────────────
bins_df <- as.data.frame(bins_gr) |>
  transmute(
    chrom  = as.character(seqnames),
    start  = start,
    end    = end,
    mid    = (start + end) / 2,
    fire   = bins_fire$score,   # FIRE percent accessible (from all.percent.accessible.bw)
    m6a    = m6a,               # (fire_cov + linker_cov) / total_cov
    cpg    = bins_cpg$score / 100,    # CpG methylation fraction derived from percent
    nuc    = nuc_frac           # nuc_cov / total_cov
  )

## Peaks for rug marks
peaks_df <- as.data.frame(peaks_gr) |>
  transmute(
    mid        = (start + end) / 2,
    fire_score = .data$score
  )
```

```{r genome_wide_plot, fig.width=14, fig.height=7, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## ── What this plot shows ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Three stacked genome-browser-style tracks along the selected chromosome,
## each summarised in 10 kb non-overlapping bins:
##
##  TRACK 1 — m6A fraction (purple)
##    The fraction of PacBio CCS reads at each genomic bin whose underlying DNA
##    is marked by m6A methylation (i.e. nucleosome-free, accessible chromatin).
##    Derived from the FIRE pipeline coverage bigwigs:
##      (all.fire.coverage.bw score + all.linker.coverage.bw score)
##      / (all.fire.coverage.bw score + all.linker.coverage.bw score
##         + all.nucleosome.coverage.bw score)
##    High m6A = open, accessible chromatin; low m6A = packed nucleosomes.
##
##  TRACK 2 — CpG methylation / 5mC (green)
##    `*_CPG.combined.bw` stores CpG methylation as a percentage-like score.
##    The code converts it to a fraction with `bins_cpg$score / 100`, then
##    summarizes it into 10 kb bins via `binnedAverage()`.
##    High 5mC = silenced / constitutive heterochromatin (e.g. gene deserts,
##    repeat-rich regions); low 5mC = actively regulated or gene-rich regions.
##
##  TRACK 3 — Nucleosome fraction (grey)
##    The complement of m6A fraction: proportion of reads carrying a positioned
##    nucleosome at each bin (nucleosome coverage / total coverage).
##    Inversely correlated with m6A; peaks here correspond to troughs in track 1.
##
##  RUG MARKS (orange, bottom of each panel)
##    Positions of called FIRE peaks — loci where a statistically significant
##    fraction of single molecules simultaneously show open chromatin
##    (recurrent accessibility across the cell population).

bins_long <- bins_df |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(m6a, cpg, nuc),   # FIRE % accessible removed (redundant with m6A)
                      names_to  = "track",
                      values_to = "value") |>
  arrange(track, mid) |>
  group_by(track) |>
  mutate(
    value_smooth = data.table::frollmean(
      value,
      n = 11L,
      align = "center",
      fill = NA_real_,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(track = factor(track,
                        levels = c("m6a", "cpg", "nuc"),
                        labels = c("m6A fraction\n(open chromatin)",
                                   "CpG methylation fraction\n(5mC)",
                                   "Nucleosome\nfraction")))

track_colors <- c(
  "m6A fraction\n(open chromatin)" = "#756BB1",
  "CpG methylation fraction\n(5mC)" = "#31A354",
  "Nucleosome\nfraction"           = "#636363"
)

plot_title <- paste0(
  "Single-molecule chromatin state landscape along ", plot_chr,
  "  |  ", sample,
  "  (", BIN_SIZE / 1e3, " kb bins)"
)

gw_plot <- ggplot(bins_long, aes(x = mid / 1e6, y = value, colour = track)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.15, alpha = 0.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(aes(y = value_smooth), linewidth = 0.7, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_rug(data = peaks_df,
           aes(x = mid / 1e6, alpha = fire_score),
           inherit.aes = FALSE,
           colour = "#E6550D", linewidth = 0.25,
           sides  = "b", length = unit(0.06, "npc"),
           show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = track_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name   = paste0(plot_chr, " position (Mb)"),
    labels = label_number(suffix = " Mb", scale = 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(3)) +
  facet_wrap(~ track, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y", strip.position = "left") +
  labs(
    title    = plot_title,
    subtitle = "Orange rug marks = FIRE peaks (population-level recurrent accessibility)",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(size = 9, angle = 0, hjust = 1),
    strip.placement   = "outside",
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "mm"),
    axis.text.x       = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.y       = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11)
  )

print(gw_plot)

dir.create("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/FIRE_genome_tracks", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave(
  file.path("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/FIRE_genome_tracks",
            paste0(sample, "_", plot_chr, "_tracks.pdf")),
  gw_plot,
  width = 14, height = 8, units = "in"
)
```

# inspect pb-CpG tools output

```{r bw-plot-setup}
library(Gviz)
library(rtracklayer)
library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# --- variables ---
sample <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
chr    <- "chr1"
total_len <- seqlengths(Hsapiens)[chr]

fire_bw_dir <- file.path(
  "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results",
  sample, "trackHub-v0.1/bw"
)
cpg_bw_dir <- file.path(
  "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/fire_CpG", sample
)

# bigwig paths and per-track colours (no hap tracks)
bw_files <- list(
  fire_coverage       = file.path(fire_bw_dir, "all.fire.coverage.bw"),
  linker_coverage     = file.path(fire_bw_dir, "all.linker.coverage.bw"),
  nucleosome_coverage = file.path(fire_bw_dir, "all.nucleosome.coverage.bw"),
  pct_accessible      = file.path(fire_bw_dir, "all.percent.accessible.bw"),
  cpg_combined        = file.path(cpg_bw_dir,  paste0(sample, "_CPG.combined.bw"))
)

track_colors <- c(
  fire_coverage       = "#E41A1C",  # red
  linker_coverage     = "#FF7F00",  # orange
  nucleosome_coverage = "#4DAF4A",  # green
  pct_accessible      = "#984EA3",  # purple
  cpg_combined        = "#377EB8"   # blue
)

# simple genome axis + one DataTrack per bigwig
axis_track <- GenomeAxisTrack()

data_tracks <- mapply(function(name, path, color) {
  DataTrack(
    range         = path,
    genome        = "hg38",
    chromosome    = chr,
    name          = name,
    type          = "histogram",
    col.histogram = color,
    fill.histogram = color,
    ylim          = if (name == "cpg_combined") c(0, 500) else NULL
  )
}, names(bw_files), bw_files, track_colors, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)



# plot first 5 Mb of chr1 as a quick overview
pdf("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/FIRE_genome_tracks/bw_track_plot_AL10_bc2178_19130_chr1_100Mb_150Mb.pdf",
    width = 10, height = 6)

plotTracks(
  c(list(axis_track), data_tracks),
  chromosome = chr,
  from = 100e6,
  to = 150e6,
  main = paste(sample, chr)
)

dev.off()
```

```{r cpg-structure}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

file <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/fire_CpG/AL10_bc2178_19130/AL10_bc2178_19130_CPG.combined.bed.gz"

dt <- fread(
  cmd = paste0("zcat '", file, "' | grep -v '^##'"),
  sep = "\t",
  header = TRUE
)
# The header line is "#chrom ..." — strip the leading '#' from the first column
setnames(dt, "#chrom", "chrom")

str(dt)
head(dt)
```

```{r cpg-qc-plots, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
# 1. Distribution of continuous mod_score
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(patchwork) 

# 1. mod_score distribution
p1 <- ggplot(dt, aes(x = mod_score)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "steelblue", color = NA) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +
  labs(
    title = "mod_score distribution (BW signal)",
    x = "mod_score (0-100)", y = "# CpG sites"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

# 2. discretized_mod_score
p2 <- ggplot(dt, aes(x = discretized_mod_score)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "coral", color = NA) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +
  labs(
    title = "discretized_mod_score distribution",
    x = "discretized_mod_score", y = "# CpG sites"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

# 3. Coverage distribution
p3 <- ggplot(dt[cov <= 100], aes(x = cov)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "forestgreen", color = NA) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +
  labs(
    title = "Coverage per CpG site",
    x = "Coverage", y = "# CpG sites"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

# Combine plots (auto-scaled y-axis for each)
p_qc <- (p1 / p2 / p3)  # vertical stack

# Save
ggsave(
  "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/pg_cpg/cpg_qc_AL10_bc2178_19130.png",
  plot   = p_qc,
  width  = 10,
  height = 15
)


```

```{r cpg-high-methylation-summary, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
summary_tbl <- data.table(
  Metric = c(
    "discretized_mod_score == 100",
    "mod_score > 90",
    "mod_score > 80",
    "Coverage == 4 (min-cov)",
    "cov == 4 AND disc_score == 100"
  ),
  Pct = c(
    mean(dt$discretized_mod_score == 100),
    mean(dt$mod_score > 90),
    mean(dt$mod_score > 80),
    mean(dt$cov == 4),
    mean(dt$cov == 4 & dt$discretized_mod_score == 100)
  )
)

p_bar <- ggplot(summary_tbl, aes(x = reorder(Metric, Pct), y = Pct * 100)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Pct * 100, 1), "%")),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 105), labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  labs(
    title = "CpG site summary: fraction meeting each criterion",
    x = NULL, y = "% of all CpG sites"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggsave(
  "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/pg_cpg/cpg_summary_barplot.pdf",
  plot = p_bar, width = 8, height = 5
)

p_bar
knitr::kable(summary_tbl[, .(Metric, `% of Sites` = paste0(round(Pct * 100, 1), "%"))],
             align = c("l", "r"))
```

# Coverage QC: why raw counts drop at the edges in the per-read plot
############################################################################################

The per-read normalized meta-profile drops at ±2 kb because reads must physically
span from the TSS to that distance to contribute nucleosomes there. Fewer long reads
reach the edges, so the denominator (total read-TSS pairs) is constant but the
numerator shrinks. The raw midpoint plot (CAGE_TSS_nucleosome_midpoints_raw.pdf)
does not suffer from this because each nucleosome midpoint is projected independently
to any TSS window it falls in — no read-length constraint.

## Step 1 — Reproduce {r CAGE_TSS} data

```{r coverage_qc_setup_raw}
source("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/plot_nuc_distribution_functions.R")

tss_path_qc    <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations/fantom5/fantom5.hg38.LCL.consensus.CAGE_peaks.bed.gz"
sample_root_qc <- "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results"
sample_qc      <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
chrom_qc       <- "chr1"
window_bp_qc   <- 2000L
bin_size_qc    <- 10L

# locate chr1 BED12
nuc_dir_qc <- file.path(sample_root_qc, sample_qc, "extracted_results/nuc_by_chr")
bed_files_qc <- list.files(nuc_dir_qc,
                            pattern = "ft_extracted_nuc\\.chr.*\\.bed\\.gz$",
                            full.names = TRUE)
bed_file_qc <- bed_files_qc[grepl(paste0("\\.", chrom_qc, "\\."), bed_files_qc)][1]
stopifnot(!is.na(bed_file_qc), file.exists(bed_file_qc))

bed12_qc <- fread(bed_file_qc, header = FALSE,
                  col.names = c("chrom","chromStart","chromEnd","name","score","strand",
                                "thickStart","thickEnd","itemRgb",
                                "blockCount","blockSizes","blockStarts"))

nucs_qc   <- calculate_nucleosome_midpoints(bed12_qc)
nuc_gr_qc <- nuc_midpoints_to_granges(nucs_qc)
tss_gr_qc <- load_tss_annotations_bed9(tss_path_qc)

# raw midpoint meta (same as CAGE_TSS chunk)
plot_dt_qc <- find_nucs_near_tss(nuc_gr_qc, tss_gr_qc, window_bp = window_bp_qc)
plot_dt_qc[, bin := round(rel_pos / bin_size_qc) * bin_size_qc]

meta_raw_qc <- plot_dt_qc[, .(
  N_raw        = .N,
  n_tss_raw    = uniqueN(tss_pos)   # how many distinct TSS contributed to this bin
), by = bin][order(bin)]

cat(sprintf("Raw method: %d bins, total nuc-TSS hits = %d\n",
            nrow(meta_raw_qc), sum(meta_raw_qc$N_raw)))
cat(sprintf("Number of TSS in window: %d\n", length(tss_gr_qc)))
```
Number of TSS in window: 111640
Raw method: 401 bins, total nuc-TSS hits = 3994365


## Step 2 — Reproduce {r CAGE_TSS_per_read} data

```{r coverage_qc_setup_per_read}
source("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/plot_nuc_distribution_functions.R")

# (same paths as above — already in memory, just restate for clarity)
tss_path_qc    <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations/fantom5/fantom5.hg38.LCL.consensus.CAGE_peaks.bed.gz"
sample_root_qc <- "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results"
sample_qc      <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
chrom_qc       <- "chr1"
window_bp_qc   <- 2000L
bin_size_qc    <- 10L

nuc_dir_qc <- file.path(sample_root_qc, sample_qc, "extracted_results/nuc_by_chr")
bed_files_qc <- list.files(nuc_dir_qc,
                            pattern = "ft_extracted_nuc\\.chr.*\\.bed\\.gz$",
                            full.names = TRUE)
bed_file_qc <- bed_files_qc[grepl(paste0("\\.", chrom_qc, "\\."), bed_files_qc)][1]

bed12_qc <- fread(bed_file_qc, header = FALSE,
                  col.names = c("chrom","chromStart","chromEnd","name","score","strand",
                                "thickStart","thickEnd","itemRgb",
                                "blockCount","blockSizes","blockStarts"))
tss_gr_qc <- load_tss_annotations_bed9(tss_path_qc)

# per-read raw meta — already returns n_reads and n_tss per bin
meta_per_read_qc <- make_meta_per_read_raw(
  bed12_qc, tss_gr_qc,
  window_bp = window_bp_qc,
  bin_size  = bin_size_qc,
  label     = "CAGE TSS"
)

cat(sprintf("Per-read method: %d bins, total nuc-read-TSS hits = %d\n",
            nrow(meta_per_read_qc), sum(meta_per_read_qc$N)))

# also compute read spans for coverage model
c <- bed12_qc[, .(
  RID        = name,
  read_start = chromStart,
  read_end   = chromEnd,
  read_len   = chromEnd - chromStart
)]
cat(sprintf("Read length: median = %d bp, mean = %d bp, max = %d bp\n",
            as.integer(median(c$read_len)),
            as.integer(mean(c$read_len)),
            max(c$read_len)))
```

Per-read method: 401 bins, total nuc-read-TSS hits = 2377156
Read length: median = 10552 bp, mean = 11271 bp, max = 50859 bp



## Step 3 — QC plots: coverage by distance

```{r coverage_qc_plots, fig.width=10, fig.height=12}
out_dir_qc <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/nuc/coverage_qc"
dir.create(out_dir_qc, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ── QC 1: n_reads per bin in the per-read method ──────────────────────────────
p_nreads <- ggplot(meta_per_read_qc, aes(x = bin, y = n_reads)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, color = "#2ca02c") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_qc, window_bp_qc, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x     = "Distance to TSS",
    y     = "Number of unique reads contributing",
    title = "Per-read method: reads per bin",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chr: %s | Shows coverage drop at edges",
                       sample_qc, chrom_qc)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_nreads)

# ── QC 2: n_tss per bin in the raw method ─────────────────────────────────────
p_ntss_raw <- ggplot(meta_raw_qc, aes(x = bin, y = n_tss_raw)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_qc, window_bp_qc, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x     = "Distance to TSS",
    y     = "Number of distinct TSS contributing",
    title = "Raw method: distinct TSS per bin",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chr: %s | Flat = no TSS-coverage bias",
                       sample_qc, chrom_qc)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_ntss_raw)

# ── QC 3: n_tss per bin in per-read method ────────────────────────────────────
p_ntss_perread <- ggplot(meta_per_read_qc, aes(x = bin, y = n_tss)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, color = "#d62728") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_qc, window_bp_qc, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x     = "Distance to TSS",
    y     = "Number of distinct TSS contributing",
    title = "Per-read method: distinct TSS per bin",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chr: %s | Also drops at edges", sample_qc, chrom_qc)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_ntss_perread)

# ── QC 4: overlay raw N for both methods (scaled to compare shape) ────────────
# scale each to max=1 to compare the shape of coverage drop
meta_raw_qc[,      N_scaled := N_raw / max(N_raw, na.rm = TRUE)]
meta_per_read_qc[, N_scaled := N    / max(N,    na.rm = TRUE)]

meta_compare <- rbindlist(list(
  meta_raw_qc[,      .(bin, N_scaled, method = "Raw midpoints")],
  meta_per_read_qc[, .(bin, N_scaled, method = "Per-read raw")]
))

p_compare <- ggplot(meta_compare, aes(x = bin, y = N_scaled, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Raw midpoints" = "steelblue", "Per-read raw" = "#2ca02c")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_qc, window_bp_qc, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x     = "Distance to TSS",
    y     = "Scaled N (max = 1)",
    color = "Method",
    title = "Shape comparison: raw midpoints vs per-read",
    subtitle = "Both scaled to max=1 to isolate edge-drop effect"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_compare)

# ── QC 5: reads per bin vs n_tss per bin in per-read method (should track) ───
p_reads_vs_tss <- ggplot(meta_per_read_qc, aes(x = bin)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = n_reads, color = "n_reads"), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(y = n_tss,   color = "n_tss"),   linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("n_reads" = "#2ca02c", "n_tss" = "#d62728")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_qc, window_bp_qc, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x     = "Distance to TSS",
    y     = "Count",
    color = NULL,
    title = "Per-read method: reads vs TSS contributing per bin",
    subtitle = "If n_reads drops more steeply than n_tss → long reads are scarce at the edges"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_reads_vs_tss)

# ── QC 5: distinct TSS per bin in per-read and raw methods ──────────────────────────────
#      bin     N n_reads n_tss   source
#    <num> <int>   <int> <int>   <char>
# 1: -2000   696     289   657 CAGE TSS
# 2: -1990   987     345   910 CAGE TSS
# 3: -1980  1137     392  1066 CAGE TSS
# 4: -1970   931     346   899 CAGE TSS
# 5: -1960  1289     410  1190 CAGE TSS
# 6: -1950  1036     363   979 CAGE TSS



distinct_tss_compare <- rbindlist(list(
  meta_raw_qc[,      .(bin, N = n_tss_raw, method = "Raw midpoints")],
  meta_per_read_qc[, .(bin, N = n_tss,     method = "Per-read raw")]
))

# head(distinct_tss_compare)
#      bin     N        method
#    <num> <int>        <char>
# 1: -2000  4687 Raw midpoints
# 2: -1990  6212 Raw midpoints
# 3: -1980  6952 Raw midpoints
# 4: -1970  6248 Raw midpoints
# 5: -1960  6960 Raw midpoints
# 6: -1950  6219 Raw midpoints

# histogram of n_tss per bin for both methods
p_hist_ntss <- ggplot(distinct_tss_compare, aes(x = N, fill = method)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.5, bins = 30) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Raw midpoints" = "red", "Per-read raw" = "blue")) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of distinct TSS contributing to bin",
    y = "# bins",
    fill = "Method",
    title = "Distribution of distinct TSS per bin for both methods"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_hist_ntss)

# save all QC plots
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_qc, sprintf("%s_%s_qc_nreads_per_bin.pdf",       sample_qc, chrom_qc)), plot = p_nreads,       width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_qc, sprintf("%s_%s_qc_ntss_raw_per_bin.pdf",     sample_qc, chrom_qc)), plot = p_ntss_raw,     width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_qc, sprintf("%s_%s_qc_ntss_perread_per_bin.pdf", sample_qc, chrom_qc)), plot = p_ntss_perread, width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_qc, sprintf("%s_%s_qc_method_shape_compare.pdf", sample_qc, chrom_qc)), plot = p_compare,      width = 7, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_qc, sprintf("%s_%s_qc_reads_vs_tss.pdf",         sample_qc, chrom_qc)), plot = p_reads_vs_tss, width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_qc, sprintf("%s_%s_qc_hist_ntss_compare.pdf",     sample_qc, chrom_qc)), plot = p_hist_ntss,    width = 6, height = 4)

cat("QC plots saved to:", out_dir_qc, "\n")
```

# Per-read coverage-normalised CAGE TSS meta-profile
#
# Problem: make_meta_per_read_raw() divides the total nucleosome count per bin
# by a single global value (total read-TSS pairs), so bins near ±2 kb receive
# the same denominator as bins near 0, even though far fewer reads physically
# span that distance. The result is an artificial drop in apparent nucleosome
# density at the window edges.
#
# Fix: divide each bin's raw count (N) by the number of UNIQUE READS that
# actually contributed nucleosomes to that bin (n_reads). This converts the
# y-axis from "total nuc-read-TSS triplets" to "mean nucleosomes per read at
# this position", with a denominator that reflects actual coverage at each bin.

```{r per_read_coverage_normalised}
source("/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/plot_nuc_distribution_functions.R")

tss_path_norm    <- "/project/spott/cshan/annotations/fantom5/fantom5.hg38.LCL.consensus.CAGE_peaks.bed.gz"
sample_root_norm <- "/project/spott/1_Shared_projects/LCL_Fiber_seq/FIRE/results"
sample_norm      <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
chrom_norm       <- "chr1"
window_bp_norm   <- 2000L
bin_size_norm    <- 10L

# ── Load data (same as {r CAGE_TSS} and {r CAGE_TSS_per_read}) ──────────────
nuc_dir_norm <- file.path(sample_root_norm, sample_norm, "extracted_results/nuc_by_chr")
bed_files_norm <- list.files(nuc_dir_norm,
                              pattern = "ft_extracted_nuc\\.chr.*\\.bed\\.gz$",
                              full.names = TRUE)
bed_file_norm <- bed_files_norm[grepl(paste0("\\.", chrom_norm, "\\."), bed_files_norm)][1]
stopifnot(!is.na(bed_file_norm), file.exists(bed_file_norm))

bed12_norm <- fread(bed_file_norm, header = FALSE,
                    col.names = c("chrom","chromStart","chromEnd","name","score","strand",
                                  "thickStart","thickEnd","itemRgb",
                                  "blockCount","blockSizes","blockStarts"))

tss_gr_norm <- load_tss_annotations_bed9(tss_path_norm)

# ── Raw per-read counts (already carries n_reads per bin) ───────────────────
# make_meta_per_read_raw() returns:
#   bin     : 10 bp bin centre (relative to TSS)
#   N       : total (nucleosome × read × TSS) triplets in this bin
#   n_reads : number of distinct reads contributing to this bin
#   n_tss   : number of distinct TSS contributing to this bin
meta_raw_norm <- make_meta_per_read_raw(
  bed12_norm, tss_gr_norm,
  window_bp = window_bp_norm,
  bin_size  = bin_size_norm,
  label     = "CAGE TSS"
)

# ── Coverage normalisation: N_per_read = N / n_reads ─────────────────────────
# For each 10 bp bin, divide the raw count by however many unique reads
# contributed a nucleosome to that bin.  Bins near ±2 kb have low n_reads
# (only long reads reach there), so this corrects for the coverage gradient
# instead of spreading that gradient into the signal.
meta_raw_norm[, N_per_read := N / n_reads]

# ── Plot 1: raw per-read counts (uncorrected) ────────────────────────────────
p_uncorrected <- ggplot(meta_raw_norm, aes(x = bin, y = N)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, color = "#2ca02c") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.05, se = FALSE,
              color = "#2ca02c", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_norm, window_bp_norm, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x        = "Distance to TSS",
    y        = "Nucleosome count (nuc × read × TSS triplets)",
    title    = "Per-read raw counts — UNCORRECTED",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chr: %s | drops at edges = read-length bias",
                       sample_norm, chrom_norm)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_uncorrected)

# ── Plot 2: coverage-normalised (N / n_reads per bin) ────────────────────────
p_corrected <- ggplot(meta_raw_norm, aes(x = bin, y = N_per_read)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, color = "#d62728") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.05, se = FALSE,
              color = "#d62728", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_norm, window_bp_norm, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x        = "Distance to TSS",
    y        = "Mean nucleosomes per contributing read (per 10 bp bin)",
    title    = "Per-read coverage-normalised — CORRECTED",
    subtitle = sprintf("Sample: %s | Chr: %s | N / n_reads per bin",
                       sample_norm, chrom_norm)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_corrected)

# ── Plot 3: side-by-side comparison (both scaled to max = 1) ─────────────────
meta_compare_norm <- rbindlist(list(
  meta_raw_norm[, .(bin, value = N        / max(N,         na.rm = TRUE), method = "Uncorrected (N)")],
  meta_raw_norm[, .(bin, value = N_per_read / max(N_per_read, na.rm = TRUE), method = "Corrected (N / n_reads)")]
))

p_compare_norm <- ggplot(meta_compare_norm, aes(x = bin, y = value, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Uncorrected (N)"        = "#2ca02c",
    "Corrected (N / n_reads)" = "#d62728"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_norm, window_bp_norm, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x        = "Distance to TSS",
    y        = "Scaled signal (max = 1)",
    color    = NULL,
    title    = "Effect of coverage normalisation on per-read meta-profile",
    subtitle = "Both scaled to max=1 to compare shape only"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_compare_norm)

# ── Plot 4: n_reads per bin (the coverage curve being corrected for) ──────────
p_coverage_curve <- ggplot(meta_raw_norm, aes(x = bin, y = n_reads)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey40") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-window_bp_norm, window_bp_norm, 500),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")) +
  labs(
    x        = "Distance to TSS",
    y        = "n_reads (unique reads contributing to bin)",
    title    = "Read coverage by distance — the correction factor",
    subtitle = "Dividing N by this curve removes the read-length gradient"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_coverage_curve)

# ── Save outputs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
out_dir_norm <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/nuc/coverage_normalised"
dir.create(out_dir_norm, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(file.path(out_dir_norm,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_per_read_uncorrected.pdf",  sample_norm, chrom_norm)),
       plot = p_uncorrected,   width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_norm,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_per_read_corrected.pdf",    sample_norm, chrom_norm)),
       plot = p_corrected,     width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_norm,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_per_read_shape_compare.pdf", sample_norm, chrom_norm)),
       plot = p_compare_norm,  width = 7, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir_norm,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_per_read_coverage_curve.pdf", sample_norm, chrom_norm)),
       plot = p_coverage_curve, width = 6, height = 4)

# Save the data table with both raw and normalised columns
fwrite(meta_raw_norm,
       file.path(out_dir_norm,
                 sprintf("%s_%s_per_read_coverage_normalised_meta.tsv",
                         sample_norm, chrom_norm)),
       sep = "\t")

cat("Coverage-normalised outputs saved to:", out_dir_norm, "\n")
```



# Plot all chromsomes aggregated FIRE peaks

this is output from /project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/code/all_chr_aggregated_FIRE_features.r

/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/nuc/all_chr_aggregated_FIRE_features

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)

sample <- "AL10_bc2178_19130"
table_dir <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/nuc/all_chr_aggregated_FIRE_features/AL10_bc2178_19130/all_chr"
plot_dir  <- "/project/spott/cshan/fiber-seq/results/plots/nuc/all_chr_aggregated_FIRE_features"

meta_norm <- fread(file.path(
  table_dir,
  sprintf("%s_all_chr_fiber_features_norm_profiles.tsv.gz", sample)
))

meta_norm <- meta_norm[feature %in% c("Nucleosome", "m6A", "CpG")]

meta_z <- copy(meta_norm)
meta_z[, z := if (sd(N) == 0) 0 else as.numeric(scale(N)), by = feature]

feature_colors <- c(
  "Nucleosome" = "#4D4D4D",
  "m6A" = "#7B3294",
  "CpG" = "#8C510A"
)

p_no_msp <- ggplot(meta_z, aes(x = bin, y = z, color = feature)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~source, nrow = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = feature_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-2000, 2000, 500),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x / 1000, " kb")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Distance to TSS",
    y = "Z-scored normalized signal",
    color = "Feature",
    title = "Relative positioning of nucleosome, m6A, and CpG signals",
    subtitle = "MSP filtered out before z-scoring"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

ggsave(
  file.path(plot_dir, sprintf("%s_all_chr_FIRE_features_overlay_no_MSP.pdf", sample)),
  plot = p_no_msp,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)

print(p_no_msp)
```
